description: This is a style of Late Bottled Vintage that should be aged. It has great red and black fruits at this stage but will develop a more structured, dense and firm character as it matures. Give this well-made, juicy wine with its sweet black fruits at least until 2017.
title: Poças 2009 Late Bottled Vintage  (Port)

description: Give this wine a year or three in the cellar, unless you like your Pinots a little on the tough side. Tannins and earthiness currently dominate the polished raspberry and cherry flavors. If you do drink it now, decant for a few hours, but not overnight.
title: Balletto 2009 BCD Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This is a Bordeaux blend based on Cabernet Sauvignon, with a drop of Petite Sirah for color and tannins. It's rich and deep in blackberry, currant and dark chocolate flavors, and balanced with big-time tannins and a fine cut of acidity. Doesn't seem like an ager despite the astringency, so drink it soon with rich meats and cheeses.
title: Reid Family Vineyards 2009 Bay's Blend Red (Napa Valley)

description: Subdued on the nose, this has a good concentration of fruit and a strong structure, buoyed by firm acidity. The finish is long and lingering.
title: Bella Piazza 2009 Barbera (Shenandoah Valley (CA))

description: A classic whiff of green bell peppery meets the nose along with passion fruit and honeyed aromas. The palate is fresh but has some weight, a possible reflection of the use of American Davis clones, which produce fatter wines than French clones. Beyond that look for melon, nectarine and tropical primary and finishing flavors.
title: Cono Sur 2009 Visión Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: From 1800-foot vineyards, this is a crisp, cool, simply fruity wine that has great citrus and lemon character. Very aromatic, with green herb and fresh grapefruit zest over apple juice.
title: Niepoort 2009 Tiara White (Douro)

description: While the wine is very dense, it also has layers of very ripe fruits, plums and dark berries that give it an intensely rich character. The juicy acidity is almost overwhelming, a shock of freshness as it cuts through the concentrated texture. The wine still needs to age, so drink from 2017.
title: Château Haut-Logat 2009  Haut-Médoc

description: This smells like compost. It feels hard and clacky, with sharp acidity and weedy, minty flavors that finish rough and green.
region_1: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla
title: Mano A Mano 2009 Manon Tempranillo (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla)

description: A massive wine, decadent and splendid, and a worthy followup to the near-perfect 2008. Densely packed in fruit, it explodes in blackberries, cassis, dark chocolate, minerals and sweet, toasty new oak. That richness is lifted to brilliance by the tannic structure, which is world class. So deliciously approachable, it's drinkable now, but it should develop over the next 6–8 years. Beyond that, all bets are off, because this modern style of soft, relatively high-alcohol wines has yet to prove itself over the long haul.
title: Venge 2009 Family Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Dark in color, this is a powerful, classic wine, with dense tannins. It has rich black-fruit flavors, and notes of wood and herb that are packed into its concentrated, dry structure. This is a great Douro for aging.
title: Niepoort 2009 Batuta Red (Douro)

description: A lovely Cabernet, richly attractive and deeply flavored in blackberries, blueberries and black currants. Made from vineyards scattered across Napa Valley, the wine has been blended with a splash of Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot. It shows a dramatic structure marked particularly by firm, smooth tannins, and it finishes dry. Shows the stuffing to age for 10-plus years, but it's polished enough to drink now, after decanting.
title: Yao Ming 2009 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: With some earthy aromas, this is a rich wine. It has weight, layers of wood and attractive strawberry flavor. The fruitiness is contained within a firm tannin shell that suggests the wine could age.
title: Domaine Albert Mann 2009 Clos de la Faille Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: This wine's citrus, apple, floral and vanilla flavors are dryish to off-dry. It's a soft, simple wine that will appeal to folks who like a little sweetness in the finish.
title: Loredona 2009 Pinot Grigio (Monterey)

description: Finely balanced wine, showing the warmth of 2009 as well as keeping bright peach, pink grapefruit and red berry fruit flavors. The wine shows lively final acidity for a delicious aftertaste.
title: Château les Valentines 2009 Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: This tastes distinctly sweet—like a cherry-and-chocolate confection—and the acidity is high. It's hard to predict where this is going, but it seems awkward now. Tasted twice, with consistent results.
title: Decades5 2009 Stagecoach Vineyard Petit Verdot (Atlas Peak)

description: Dry and crisp, an easy, pleasant cocktail sipper made from the great Pinot Noir grape. Shows the acidity of Monterey, and is easy to like for its spicy, herb-infused cherry and raspberry flavors.
title: Wrath 2009 Pinot Noir Saignée Rosé (Monterey)

description: Ripe and full, soft in texture and rich. The wine has spice along with the red apple, apricot and sweet strawberry flavors. The softness takes the dryness away.
title: Domaine Saint-André de Figuière 2009 Cuvée Magali Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Very dense and tannic, this wine is hiding its fruit behind its firm structure. It is solid, chunky and powerful, with plenty of weight that will always be dry. Needs to age for several years.
title: Thunevin 2009 Bad Boy  (Bordeaux)

description: Carefully aged in oak casks, this hearty Aglianico shows complexity and brawn, with bold notes of black fruit, spice and leather. Tightly firm tannins give it long staying power.
title: Mastroberardino 2009 Aglianico (Campania)

description: Opening aromas include forest floor, red berries, cooking spices and Mediterranean herbs. The juicy palate shows bright red berries, mint and thyme accented with notes of white pepper. It's well balanced but will benefit with a few more years in bottle to fully develop. Drink 2016–2020.
title: Fattoria La Lecciaia 2009  Brunello di Montalcino

description: structured Brunello, it opens with aromas of forest floor, black berry, crushed flowers and mint. The palate delivers restrained wild cherry accented with clove and Mediterranean spices alongside bracing, tightly knit tannins that need to unwind. Drink 2016–2021.
title: Castello Romitorio 2009  Brunello di Montalcino

description: A full-bodied expression of plum and cherry, it has a sanguine halo with rustic edges and a faint astringency to the finish. Made in a bigger style than other Barberas, the finish is lingering.
title: Yorba 2009 Shake Ridge Vineyards Barbera (Amador County)

description: Fresh and lively, this Cabernet Sauvignon (blended with 10% Merlot) opens with aromas of forest berry, cherry, light spice and almond. It feels streamlined and crisp, and it would pair well with cheesy pasta or lasagna.
title: Podere Guado al Melo 2009  Bolgheri

description: A little one-dimensional, but it's a very clean, elegant wine, with drily subtle cherry, Dr. Pepper cola and sandalwood flavors. Nicely tannic, with a cherryskin bitterness. Drink it now.
title: Roadhouse Winery 2009 Yellow Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Dark and smoky, this wine is relatively tannic and emphasizes flavors of leaf, bark, root and herb. There's plenty of depth and exceptional power to this wine, which seems unique among New World Tempranillos.
title: Cayuse 2009 Impulsivo En Chamberlin Vineyard Tempranillo (Walla Walla Valley (OR))

description: Apple, pear and nectarine fruit scents lead the nose of this wine, with a touch of raw almond contributing a gentle nutty accent. Medium weight with great vibrancy and freshness, a lively sweet citrus core lifts the palate and remains through the close.
title: Glen Carlou 2009 Chardonnay (Paarl)

description: This is a crisp, tonic and steely expression of Fiano that offers an incredibly focused and sharp bouquet of white rose, crushed mineral and stone fruit. The wine's precise personality is what makes it so appropriate to pair with summer pasta or seafood.
title: Mastroberardino 2009  Fiano di Avellino

description: Estate-grown and fruit-driven, this lush, pear-flavored Pinot Gris is dotted with pretty cinnamon and allspice highlights. Forward and nicely balanced, it has the texture and weight to handle rich sauces, much like a big Chardonnay, but without all the new oak.
title: Melrose 2009 Pinot Gris (Umpqua Valley)

description: From this top Bergerac producer comes this powerful blend. With its 30% Malbec, it has intense tannins and a firm, solid structure, with dense and richly concentrated blackberry fruit. It does need to age for at least five years.
title: Domaine l'Ancienne Cure 2009 L'Extase Red (Côtes de Bergerac)

description: This simple wine delivers currant, dried raspberry and ginger aromas. Medium in build and in intensity, it offers toasted accents on the finish.
title: Le Bertarole 2009  Valpolicella Ripasso Classico

description: Ultraripe black fruits—berries and cherries, along with raisins—reflect the high alcohol. Even so, the wine carries an impression of almost sugary sweetness, as if still not fermented completely dry. Spice cake and plum pudding, with heat on the finish, mark this as extreme by previous Fielding Hills standards. Drink soon.
title: Fielding Hills 2009 RiverBend Vineyard Syrah (Wahluke Slope)

description: Bold aromas of undergrowth, maduro, fruitcake and blackberry lead onto the full-bodied, assertive palate. This Bordeaux-style blend has a substantial amount of brawny tannins that crave to be paired with a hearty slab of grilled meat. The finish remains highly structured, ending on notes of espresso and dark fruit.
title: Sutcliffe 2009 Trawsfynydd Red (Colorado)

description: This is a lovely example of Ventoux Grenache (80%), with 15% Syrah and 5% a blend of Mourvèdre, Cinsaut and Carignan. It's slightly warm in style and supple in structure, but with hints of dried flowers and licorice to give verve and dimension to the black cherry fruit. Drink now and over the next few years.
title: Martinelle 2009 Red (Ventoux)

description: This Syrah-based cuvée is aged in barrel, but the cedary notes are only a backdrop to the floral, garrigue notes and dark fruit aromas. In the mouth, the flavors seem brighter and more like red berries, with plenty of savory spice for interest. It's drinking well now, but the moderate tannins suggest it should hold through 2016.
title: Mas des Bressades 2009 Cuvée Excellence Red (Costières de Nîmes)

description: Notshowing much generosity now, pinched by tough tannins that give it a scoury astringency. Deep inside is a molten core of cherries and cola, with complex earth, mushroom and mineral notes. Pretty soft, but dry and focused, and could age well. Try after 2014.
title: Aiken 2009 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)

description: The immediate impression is that this wine is heavy, ripe and oaky. Sawdust and wood grain are the lead aromas. In the mouth, this chunky Priorat is hard, with grating tannins. Hickory and toasted berry flavors finish hot and resiny.
title: Vall Llach 2009 Red (Priorat)

description: Simple, soft and fruity-sweet in cherries and raspberries, like the spicy filling in a pie. A direct wine to drink now.
title: Presqu'ile 2009 Presqu'ile Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)

description: A steely wine, its acidity dominating the attractive toast, white fruits and grapefruit flavors. It is rich, full in the mouth, a wine that shows some opulence while also preserving a firm, strict structure.
title: Olivier Leflaive 2009  Chassagne-Montrachet

description: This is a splendid rendition of Chateau Ste. Michelle's mainstay Cabernet, rich and round, with a sophisticated mix of grapes, including Malbec, Cab Franc, Petit Verdot and Syrah. Dark fruit flavors of black cherry and cassis create a lush center, and the wine is more evolved and smoother, with less obvious oak and barrel flavor, than the pricier Cabs from Ste. Michelle.
title: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2009 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A soft wine, favoring warm fruit, pear and peach flavors, the minerality coming later. The wine is crisp and fresh, with an intensity of acidity rare in 2009. That suggests it will age, although it is already fruity and refreshing.
title: Domaine Emmanuel Giboulot 2009 Les Pierres Blanches  (Côte de Beaune)

description: A 100% Petit Verdot is a rare event. Here in Bairrada, close to the ocean, the grape produces a structured, dense wine with serious blackberry fruits and dark tannins. It is deeply colored, ripe with bold fruitiness as well as acidity. Drink the wine now.
title: Campolargo 2009 Diga? Tinto Petit Verdot (Bairrada)

description: Technically proficient and showing a certain amount of style, this is a focused Cabernet with tart, tangy cassis and berry flavors. The barrel regimen adds bits of toast and mocha to the finish.
title: North by Northwest 2009 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This is a nice Chardonnay to drink with shellfish, particularly grilled shrimp. It's dry and crisp in acidity, with rich pineapple, green apple and pear flavors, accented with sweet, toasty oak.
title: Valley of the Moon 2009 Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: Rounded and soft, with gentle tannins. Packed with black cherry fruits, a herbal edge and sweetness, this is already an approachable wine, fruity and generous.
title: Joseph Drouhin 2009  Santenay

description: Fresh and bright, this wine is rich with wild berries, cassis and white cherry aromas that would pair perfectly with home-cooked pasta and meat dishes. It's natural acidity will cut through the rich consistency of those foods.
title: La Poderina 2009  Rosso di Montalcino

description: “Il Bugiardo,” which is Italian for “the liar,” is an honest and plump wine, with soft berry aromas that are woven into spicy notes of clove, black pepper, leather and tobacco. The finish is velvety and smooth.
title: Buglioni 2009 Il Bugiardo  (Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso)

description: A foursquare, solid wine that shows its structure right up front. It is backed by red fruits and acids that lend a warm feeling. Full and dense, this needs to age.
title: Chanson Père et Fils 2009  Nuits-St.-Georges

description: Medium-bodied and easy drinking, this Cabernet opens with yeasty red-fruit aromas and a touch of smoky oak, before finishing clean. A very good Cabernet Sauvignon that should be fairly easy to find.
title: B.R. Cohn 2009 Silver Label Cabernet Sauvignon (North Coast)

description: Light and forward, this pure Cabernet offers pretty strawberry fruit and soft tannins. Easy drinking, it's built for near-term consumption.
title: AntoLin Cellars 2009 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: A bit thin and quite acidic, with a real bite, but otherwise it's pleasant enough, with cherry, raspberry, mocha and cinnamon flavors.
title: Cameron Hughes 2009 Lot 251 G-S-M (Arroyo Seco)

description: A little sweet and simple, but for the price, a good approximation of Pinot Noir. It's rich in raspberry and cherry jam flavor.
title: Bogle 2009 Pinot Noir (California)

description: Quite a good Syrah, dry and robust and full bodied. It has very rich, deep flavors of blackberries, currants and coffee, as well as thick, astringent tannins that make the mouth all sticky. Drink now, with decanting, and over the next six years.
title: Dierberg 2009 Estate Grown Syrah (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Dark and saturated in color, this has earthy and meaty aromas like bloody, rare steak and charred bone—that sort of proteinous spirit. The actual fruit flavor is incredibly rich and concentrated in blackberries, black currants and crisp bacon. The impression is of a sweet, delicious Cabernet that's a touch warm in alcohol, but lush and refined.
title: Chappellet 2009 Pritchard Hill Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: A fresh style of wine, with an attractive edge of softness followed by the brightest fresh acidity. A simple, crisp, clean wine to drink now. Screwcap.
title: Manfred Tement 2009 Green Grün White (Südsteiermark)

description: Big, toasty and brooding at first, this calms down and becomes a glass of pleasing dark fruits and soft tannins, with a lilt of spice. A fine midweek wine that will pair with a range of meats and taste perfectly good on its own.
title: Graziano 2009 Syrah (Mendocino)

description: Light, fresh and easy wine, with pear and green apple fruit and a deft, soft texture. Totally ready to drink.
title: Château d'Orschwihr 2009 Bollenberg Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: A new offering from the country's largest producer of Rieslings, this fits an emerging off-dry category, with 11% alcohol and 4.7% residual sugar. It's a slightly sweeter companion to the winery's popular Columbia Valley bottling, with more of the honey and tea, less of the cool fruit flavors.
title: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2009 Harvest Select Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Made entirely with Negroamaro, this opens with aromas of underbrush and roasted coffee bean. The mature palate seems older than it is, showing prune, raisin, espresso, and licorice alongside astringent, fleeting tannins. Almost all the fruit richness has dried up.
title: Agricole Vallone 2009 Vereto Riserva Negroamaro (Salice Salentino)

description: This is an earthy Mencía with cheesy aromas, tobacco hints and a pinch of compost that suggests ripeness and maturity. The palate is deep and intense, with a modest but pointed beam of acidity. Fern, juniper, mint leaf, licorice and blackberry flavors finish warm. Drink through 2016.
title: Godelia 2009 Mencía (Bierzo)

description: A Prosecco style, with residual sugar at 12% and alcohol down around 6.5%. Just a little on the sugary side, though the acidity helps cut through, and the light spritz gives it a refreshing tingle. Fine for sipping chilled in the hot weather.
title: Silvan Ridge 2009 Semi-Sparkling Early Muscat (Oregon)

description: The Ogier firm, which controls this property, has made great strides recently. This is full bodied and silky in texture without being overly soft, and it boasts complex, well-integrated, interleaving notes of dark fruit, mocha, spice and earth. Drink 2015–2025.
title: Clos de L'Oratoire des Papes 2009 Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Big tannins give this wine a powerful structure. It has a dense texture that is slowly revealing the sweet-tasting fruit. Powerful and concentrated, it has a long future.
title: Château le Bon Pasteur 2009  Pomerol

description: A firm and very structured wine, this is for long-term aging. At this young stage, the fruit seems extracted, but this will pass as the weight of the dense damson skin and tannic character shines through. Complex and finally ripe.
title: Duorum 2009 Vintage  (Port)

description: Cesanese del Piglio is at the center of Lazio's wine renaissance, which is currently only in its initial stirrings. This is perfumed, bright and loaded with dried herb, ginger, forest fruit and spice flavors. The close is velvety and soft.
title: Casale della Ioria 2009 Torre del Piano Superiore Riserva  (Cesanese del Piglio)

description: This smooth, wood-aged wine demonstrates the acid of Zweigelt, plus complex toast, red cherry and dark chocolate flavors. The wine is rich, ripe and full-bodied, finishing with fresh juicy acidity.
title: Winzer Krems 2009 Edition Chremisa Sandgrube 13 Barrique Zweigelt (Niederösterreich)

description: Produced only in top years, this Tannat-dominated wine is named after the Greenwich 0-degree longitude that passes through the vineyard. The wine itself is powerful and structured while keeping a great sense of style. Black fruits push through strongly. offering richness and balanced acidity. The wood aging combines easily with the fruit tannins to give a textured, dry background. Drink from late 2015.
title: Château Peyros 2009 Cuvée Greenwich Tannat (Madiran)

description: Deep, compact and rich on the nose, with a big dose of ripe berry aromas. The palate is healthy and blends a beefy, muscular body with balancing acids and firm but ripe tannins. Tastes pretty and sweet, like blackberry or plum pie. Chewy and chocolaty on the finish; overall it's a winner.
title: Zuccardi 2009 Serie A Bonarda (Mendoza)

description: This wine continues a long-standing tradition, showing a mastery of the art of blending. It's made using grapes that are sourced from at least six vineyards scattered from Yountville to St. Helena. Right out of the bottle, it's a soft, smoothly tannic wine that's rich in blackberry jam, black currant, blueberry, raspberry, dark chocolate and spice flavors. The wine, which contains small amounts of Petit Verdot and Malbec, is so powerful, it easily carries the 100% new French oak. Just gorgeous now, and it should develop bottle complexity for at least the next 10 years.
title: Joseph Phelps 2009 Insignia Estate Grown Red (Napa Valley)

description: King Coal is 70% Cabernet Sauvignon and 30% Syrah from the Stoneridge Vineyard. Silky and seamless on entry, it pushes into a wine of power and punch. The fruit is complex and complicated, a mix of berry and cherry, with tart, tangy acid. While the wine is still primary, it has superb concentration and length, and it's beautifully structured for aging.
title: Charles Smith 2009 King Coal Cabernet Sauvignon-Syrah (Washington)

description: The aromas provide notes of baking spice, vanilla, licorice and dark fruit that are starting to show some bottle age. Full-bodied dried red-fruit and cranberry flavors lead to a tart finish.
title: Almquist 2009 Lacoye Ambassador Vineyard Syrah (Red Mountain)

description: Generous aromas of baking spice, clove and vanilla lead to quite tart, tangy cranberry and cherry flavors, with the oak and the fruit playing off each other.
title: Almquist 2009 Milbrant Vineyard Syrah (Wahluke Slope)

description: Fruity red berry-flavored wine, with attractive light tannins, plenty of acidity and an earthy edge. There's a touch of pepper, the finish showing fine acidity.
title: Dão Sul 2009 Cortello Tinto Red (Lisboa)

description: This Petit Verdot from southern Tuscany offers aromas of mature black fruit, oak and spice. The palate shows plum, fig, vanilla, nutmeg and oak layered with bitter chocolate. Give this a few more years to soften aggressive tannins.
title: Terre di Talamo 2009 Per Cecco Petit Verdot (Toscana)

description: This polished wine offers an array of ripe cherry, pomegranate, persimmon and cola flavors, making it rich and sweet in the mouth. Yet the finish is entirely dry, and you'll find plenty of scoury acidity to rip through the greasy richness of lamb or duck.
title: De Loach 2009 Maboroshi Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This is a lovely, gentle Syrah for drinking now with upscale fare. It's soft in both tannins and acids, making it velvety in the mouth, and the blackberry, cassis and mocha flavors are, in a word, delicious.
title: Aril 2009 Syrah (Napa Valley)

description: Aromas of cooked fruit, stewed plum, toast, coffee and vanilla lead the nose. The warm palate offers up fleshy black cherry, mocha and a licorice note, with the warmth of alcohol on the midpalate. It's already accessible so drink soon.
title: Terre Nere 2009 Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: The dusty tannins dominate this firm and dry wine. With its new wood aging and its concentration, it is complex and dense. The ripe black fruit offers power and sweetness. The wine is likely to age well over five years or more.
title: Château Lanbersac 2009 Cuvée Vieilles Vignes  (Puisseguin Saint-Émilion)

description: Thanks to its silky mouthfeel and balanced acidity, this renders a sensation of elegance and finesse. There's pulp and dimension on the nose with layers of red currant and underbrush.
title: Montaribaldi 2009 Sorì Montaribaldi  (Barbaresco)

description: This riserva shows clean, polished lines, and it offers bright nuances of cherry, blackberry, leather and moist earth. The mouthfeel is crisp but powerful, and there's a lingering sensation of forest berry and chopped herb on the finish.
title: Castellare di Castellina 2009 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: Whitcraft has produced a fine, complex young Syrah from the Stolpman Vineyard, in the emerging Ballard Canyon Region. The wine is tannic, with deep flavors of blackberries, red and black currants, grilled meat, tobacco, wild herbs, black pepper and cedar wood. While it's a bit hot in alcohol, it seems balanced enough to age, allowing the tannins and other elements resolve. Try after 2015.
title: Whitcraft 2009 Stolpman Vineyard Syrah (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Here's a clean, savory Merlot for drinking now at a fair price. It's dry, tannic and smooth in the mouth, with blackberry, blueberry and tobacco flavors, subtlely enhanced with oak.
title: Kenwood 2009 Merlot (Sonoma County)

description: This Cabernet Franc leans toward the green side, revealing aromas of cassis, green beans and leafy herbs. It feels elegant in composition, with soft red-fruit flavors and silky tannins. A mushroom note peaks on the short to medium finish.
title: Naked Mountain 2009 Cabernet Franc (Virginia)

description: A light yet concentrated Pinot Noir, this has a cherry flavor and a spicy texture. Give it some air to allow the better side of its earthy aromas time to unravel.
title: Philo Ridge 2009 Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: Clumsy and a bit chemical smelling, with a sticky, thick, candied palate housing bug juice berry flavors in front of a medicinal finish. Bulky, sweet and grabby red wine.
title: Pueblo del Sol 2009 Tannat (Juanico)

description: This single vineyard Vinho Verde is complex and powerful. The texture is creamy, rich, with hints of pineapple as well as grapefruit. There is an edgy texture, balanced with the crispest final acidity.
title: Borges 2009 Quinta de Simaens White (Vinho Verde)

description: A nice blush wine. Made from 100% Pinot Noir, it's dryish and crisp, with pretty flavors of cherries, raspberries and vanilla. Nice with chicken pot pie or chicken enchiladas.
title: Bonneau 2009 Rosé (Carneros)

description: This is heavy, soft and overripe, with raisin and Port-like flavors.
title: Bianchi 2009 Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)

description: This is a big, sweet, extracted wine, for those who like Chardonnay packed with fruit. It tastes like buttered popcorn, orange jam, gingerbread and vanilla caramel.
title: Tangley Oaks 2009 Lot #6 Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: A simple wine with tart cherry and oak flavors, and alcohol warmth. Drink now with salty, spicy meats.
title: Santa Barbara Winery 2009 Lafond Vineyard Grenache (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Tastes and feels too buttery to earn a better score, with awkward flavors of apricots and lemon peel.
title: GEN 7 2009 Reserve Chardonnay (Santa Barbara County)

description: A Côtes du Rhône-style blend meant to be drunk early and around a weekday meal, among Terre Rouge's easiest to drink and find wines. There's lots to like, from the wine's high-toned cherry and boysenberry aromas and flavors to its traces of black olive and jaunty, gamy pepper.
title: Terre Rouge 2009 Tête-á-Tête Red (Sierra Foothills)

description: A crisp wine, dry and clean, with citrus, green apple and lemongrass flavors. A small percentage of new oak adds toast, while sur lie aging brings a tart creaminess. Pretty good, but it's a little too unripe and green to earn a higher score.
title: Illumination 2009 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: Flashy aromas of toast and butter mingle with tropical fruit aromas and flavors. There's a touch of warmth on the nose, and the mouthfeel is round and fleshy. Drink now.
title: Fairvalley 2009 Chardonnay (Western Cape)

description: From a warm vintage, this Syrah is raisiny and jammy up front but more staunch and structured as it opens. Aromas of baked fruits arrive at a big, saturated palate full of blackberry, vanilla and coconut flavors. Cherry, chocolate and graphite work the finish on this ripe, warm wine. Drink through 2016.
title: Ventisquero 2009 Pangea Apalta Vineyard Syrah (Colchagua Valley)

description: Made from 100% Cabernet, this is a classic Napa Valley wine. It's dry, but the fruit is sweetly ripe, and it has firm, supple tannins and balancing acids. The rich flavors are of dark chocolate and black currants, with a heady note of summer blueberries. This will evolve over the years, but it's best enjoyed soon for its sheer deliciousness.
title: Monticello Vineyards 2009 Tietjen Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Rutherford)

description: Vall Llach is known for making ripe-styled wines, and this fits the bill. Embruix is ripe and raisiny on the nose, with black currant and prune-like aromas. A thick, jammy, stewy palate holds flavors of prune, blackberry and bitter chocolate, while the finish is hot and sticky, with molasses and chocolate flavors.
title: Vall Llach 2009 Embruix Red (Priorat)

description: Here's one of those fresh, bright Italian wines you'll want to pair with home cooked pasta or lasagna. The delivery is direct and fresh with lingering tones of cherry, root beer, licorice and sweet forest berry. The mouthfeel is clean, polished and crisp.
title: Casa di Terra 2009 Lenaia Rosso Red (Toscana)

description: Here's a solid Pinot Noir for drinking now with a fine steak or chop. It's dry, tannic and silky in the mouth, with pretty flavors of cherries and cola.
title: Schug 2009 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: La Nerthe's white is a blend of Clairette, Bourboulenc, Roussanne and Grenache Blanc. The result is a slightly floral wine that's full bodied yet lacking flesh on the midpalate. The apricot and citrus flavors finish with surprising freshness.
title: Château La Nerthe 2009 White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Soft, hot in alcohol and sweet, this simple wine tastes like melted raspberry and cherry jam.
title: The Industrial 2009 Zinfandel (Sonoma County)

description: Aromas of oak, toast, espresso, vanilla and coconut carry over to the palate along with dark berry flavors and milk chocolate. It closes on a sweet, almost sugary note but it lacks energy and intensity.
title: Ferrero 2009  Brunello di Montalcino

description: Judged against more familiar varietal wines, this Maturana comes across as unusual, but not bad. Herbal aromas of juniper and briny berry are typical for the grape. Flavors of stewed blueberry, juniper and herbs are naturally green-leaning and a bit odd. This is something to explore.
title: Dinastía Vivanco 2009 Colección Vivanco Parcelas  (Rioja)

description: This thick, dusty and leathery Foothills Zinfandel is ready to drink now with bold, meaty foods.
title: Bella Piazza 2009 Sleeper Vineyard, Old Vine Zinfandel (Shenandoah Valley (CA))

description: Dark and dense, this deep and compact Cabernet Sauvignon is saturated with cassis flavor. Accents of coffee grounds and dark chocolate add depth and power, and a hint of earthiness in the finish underscores its complexity and ageability.
title: Rulo 2009 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: The winery's Affinity bottling has had mixed results over the years, but this is one of their best, right up there with the 2007. It's a beautiful wine, absolutely dry, tannic, smooth and complex in black currant flavor. A bright streak of acidity provides wonderful balance, and should help the wine to age through at least 2019.
title: Robert Craig 2009 Affinity Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: A great pairing partner to pizzas or cheesy pastas, Tribolo has the high acidity and alcohol to wash your palate clean of fatty foods. It ends with bright notes of cherry, cassis and cola.
title: Poggio Stenti 2009 Tribolo  (Montecucco)

description: This blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot from coastal Tuscany opens with some sharp or thorny aromas of bramble or forest fruit. It delivers firm, etched tannins with a dry, polished close.
title: Cipriana 2009  Bolgheri

description: Paul Dolan has been making some great wines from his patch of biodynamic Mendocino farmland but this Zin didn't deliver the wealth of flavors many of his others have, muted aromatically and the tannins a touch stewy. Best with boldly flavored foods around the barbecue or campfire.
title: Paul Dolan 2009 Zinfandel (Mendocino County)

description: Light, bright and balanced, this inexpensive Chardonnay is not complex and could use a little oomph in there somewhere, but has enough apple and pear flavor to get you through.
title: Sharabella Wines 2009 Mery nel Cielo Chardonnay (Lodi)

description: Hot, spicy and sweet in blackberries, cherries and raspberries, like a fruit reduction sauce. The tannins are tough. Cries out for barbecue.
title: Rock Wall 2009 Carver Sutro Palisades Vineyard Petite Sirah (Napa Valley)

description: This vintage of Brancott's Sauvignon Blanc knocks it right down the center of the fairway—clean and refreshing, marked by brisk acids. Stone fruit and melon notes abound, tinged with just the right proportion of green herbs. Drink now.
title: Brancott 2009 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Slightly granular smelling and seemingly oversized on the bouquet. In the mouth it folds itself back into standard form, with earthy, jammy flavors of wild berry and spice. Seems a touch heavy and slightly green on the finish, but overall it gets the job done.
title: Alberti 154 2009 Merlot (Tupungato)

description: This wine has a juicy black-currant character that is bright, with just the right amount of balanced sweetness. It is more fruity than rich and packs a welcome balance of final acidity,
title: Sogevinus 2009 Rocha Late Bottled Vintage  (Port)

description: This is great Sancerre from the Monts Damnés vineyard. It shows all the right varietal character, stylized by initial austerity, flint and a dense texture. Gradually it opens up to show a balance of acidity and richness.
title: Henri Bourgeois 2009 Le M.D. de Bourgeois  (Sancerre)

description: A food-friendly blend of Schiava and Lagrein (two native grapes of northern Italy) this ruby-colored wine opens with a lean consistency and medium build. It shows cool-climate aromas of small forest fruit and blue flowers with a touch of white almond on the close.
title: Andriano 2009 Santa Maddalena Red (Alto Adige)

description: Drink this charming Merlot now for its soft, sweet richness of fruit. With a touch of smoky oak, it shows cherry, red currant and mocha flavors that are wrapped into polished tannins.
title: Summers 2009 Ranch Reserve Merlot (Knights Valley)

description: Lean and streamlined, this has bright aromas of dried rosemary, currant, cassis and spice. There's isn't too much pulpy fruit or extraction, but the acidity is refreshening and bright.
title: Vaona 2009 Paverno  (Amarone della Valpolicella Classico)

description: While it has all the structure of Gevrey-Chambertin, it also has the richness of the vintage. This strikes a healthy balance between the two, creating a powerful wine that's full of black fruits and sweet tannins, with a firm, dry core. Age for 5–6 years.
title: Jean-Claude Boisset 2009 Lavaut Saint-Jacques Premier Cru  (Gevrey-Chambertin)

description: Aromas of crushed minerals, blackened spices, blackberry and dark cherry are integrated and whole. The palate is ripe and tannic but not fierce, with blackberry, black cherry, toast and spice flavors that finish chocolaty and rich, with nutmeg-like notes and length. Best from 2015 through 2022.
title: Torrederos 2009 Reserva  (Ribera del Duero)

description: A fruit-driven style, with cherry and raspberry notes accented by hints of chocolate and coffee. It's ripe and creamy on the midpalate, just fading a little too quickly on the finish. Drink now–2013.
title: Domaine Brusset 2009 Les Boudalles Red (Ventoux)

description: A blend of 20% Cabernet Sauvignon and 80% Fer Servadou, this is the top cuvée from Domaine Laurens. It has a new wood richness and firm tannins, with an herbal character that blends into the blackberry fruit. Age for another year.
title: Domaine Laurens 2009 Cuvée de Flars  (Marcillac)

description: Dark and rich, this bottling is redolent of kirsch, crème de cassis, star anise and cola. The palate is framed by leathery tannins and pinpoint-like acidity that carry through to the finish, offering cola, dried black cherry and licorice flavors. A harbinger of Malibu's potential.
title: Cielo 2009 Purple Mountain Cabernet Sauvignon (Saddle Rock-Malibu)

description: This is overripe and raisiny, no two ways about it. Hailing from a very hot year, this feels flat and flabby, while raisin and prune are the dominant flavors. Blatant overripeness and a ton of weight render this out of balance.
title: Budeguer 2009 Patrimonio Seleccion de Barricas Malbec (Mendoza)

description: Vanilla, strawberry and cherry on the nose leads into a spicy but balanced blend of fresh and fruity flavors. The wine offers ample minerality so it's clean on the tongue. Delightful and flirty, and good with fruit desserts.
title: Domaine Costa Lazaridi 2009 Amethystos Rosé (Drama)

description: From the hills around Vienna, this Riesling is a rich style, emphasizing fruity lemon and apricot flavors as well as steely acidity. It's a fine combination with a tightly perfumed aftertaste. Screw cap.
title: Wieninger 2009 Vienna Hills Riesling (Vienna)

description: This expression of Fiano comes forward with crisp, clean aromas of freshly cut lemon, orange blossom and almond paste. The mouthfeel is tonic and lean, but the real point here is the wine's freshness.
title: Rocca del Principe 2009  Fiano di Avellino

description: Pretty good, sturdy and clean. The nose shows good white fruits and minerally notes, with a bit of toast. In the mouth, it's healthy and sweet, with tropical flavors of melon and papaya along with creamy pear. Finishes sweet, with some vanilla.
title: Ventisquero 2009 Reserva Chardonnay (Casablanca Valley)

description: Fruit-forward and simple, with the sugared taste of black cherry jam. Very high alcohol shows up in the form of jalapeño pepper heat.
title: Midnight 2009 Estate Merlot (Paso Robles)

description: Initial aromas are scratchy and piercing, but with airing this blend of Syrah, Garnacha and Cabernet Sauvignon offers intriguing notes of vanilla, tobacco and charcoal. In the mouth, this is undeniably thick and tannic. Flavors are saturated and suggest blackberry and cassis. The finish leans toward syrupy but maintains acidity for balance. Drink through 2018.
title: Finca Casa Lo Alto 2009 Reserva Red (Utiel-Requena)

description: Too sweet, like pineapple and pear juice sweetened with white sugar. The underlying wine is pretty good, with decent acidity and a clean minerality. But that sweetness is over the top.
title: Sebastiani 2009 Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: The Eléni Syrah, sourced from old vines at Red Willow Vineyard, may lay claim to being the best of a very fine lineup. It is a densely fruity wine, with concentrated flavors of raspberry liqueur, set upon a foundation of liquid rock. Focused and precise, it is almost crystalline in its resonant elegance.
title: Efeste 2009 Eléni Syrah (Yakima Valley)

description: A proper, textbook Pinot Noir—it's dry, nicely crisp, with smoothly astringent tannins. It has pleasant, complex cherry, pomegranate, plum, tobacco, bacon and sandalwood flavors. Not an ager, so drink up with upscale fare.
title: MacMurray Ranch 2009 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Fleshy black-plum, cherry and blackberry notes lead this medium-weight wine, with a light dusting of cocoa powder. Dark but vibrant in the mouth, with a good amount of pepper and licorice on the finish. Drink now.
title: Tormentoso 2009 Mourvèdre (Paarl)

description: This kabinett possesses lovely balance, marrying sweet pineapple aspects with zesty citrus notes. The fruit is lush to the point of slightly covering the underlying minerality, but remains clean and racy on the finish.
title: Dr. H. Thanisch (Erben Müller-Burggraef) 2009 Bernkasteler Badstube Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: Falcone wisely reins in the fruity richness and oak on this wine, allowing acidity and citrus flavors to star. That is a nod to Santa Barbara's cool-climate terroir, not winemaker interference. The wine is dry, crisp and minerally, with subtle lime, Meyer lemon and Asian pear flavors, with a hint of oak. A lovely restaurant wine at a fair price.
title: Falcone 2009 Chardonnay (Santa Barbara County)

description: Integrated aromas of blackberry, road tar and spice cake set up a palate with pounding tannins and overt tartaric acidity. Earthy blackberry and baking-spice flavors are long and lasting on the finish. This is 40% Malbec, 30% Tempranillo and 30% Syrah.
title: Antigal 2009 Aduentus Mediterráneo Red (Mendoza)

description: Candied and simple, with raspberry and cherry Lifesaver flavors. Okay for big parties.
title: flipflop 2009 Merlot (California)

description: Good price for such a lush, likeable Merlot. It's soft and rich in cherries, red currants and licorice. This is easly the best Sebastiani Merlot in many years.
title: Sebastiani 2009 Merlot (Alexander Valley)

description: A nice follow-up to the excellent '08 Zardoz, this is earthy and roasted smelling, full of cola and berry notes on the nose. It feels round and smooth, with a mild tannic bite and baked flavors of blackberry, cassis and chocolate. It's layered feeling on the finish, with a bit of syrupy residue.
title: Indomita 2009 Zardoz Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: A very good Cabernet for drinking now. It's dry and pretty tannic, with blackberry, cherry and tobacco flavors that finish spicy and seemingly sweet. It earns extra points for its chewy deliciousness.
title: Kenwood 2009 Jack London Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma Valley)

description: Full bodied and supple, this wine shows an interesting array of sensory notes for Barossa Shiraz, with notes ranging from espresso, tar, mint and green peppercorn to dark chocolate and blueberry. Finishes long and softly dusty, suggesting the ability to age through 2020, although it's very drinkable now.
title: Château Tanunda 2009 The Château 100 Year Old Vines Shiraz (Barossa)

description: Pungent of baked plum and baking spice, this Shenandoah Barbera is soft and rustic, yet refined, with a jolt of mocha espresso on the finish. The expert acidity enveloping this wine should pair with Mediterrean-themed food.
title: Easton 2009 Cooper Ranch Barbera (Shenandoah Valley (CA))

description: Here's a thin and lightweight expression from Calabria (the “toe of the boot of Italy”) that offers delicate tones of white flower and peach blossom backed by fresh crispness and zest. It's a very easygoing and informal wine overall.
title: Colacino 2009 Quarto White (Calabria)

description: Good cherry, cocoa and sweet oak flavors in this dry wine. It's too soft, lacking structure, but is a pretty good buy for the price.
title: Pennywise 2009 Merlot (California)

description: Here's a cheerful, no-fuss white blend from central Italy (with Trebbiano, Chardonnay and Sauvignon Blanc grapes) that would pair with informal rice or pasta salad. Citrus, floral and peach flavors wash over the palate.
title: Umberto Cesari 2009 Moma White (Rubicone)

description: 88-90 Barrel sample. Very soft, rounded wine, the tannins a mere hint as the fruit rolls forward. Fine, juicy spice.
title: Château Anthonic 2009 Barrel sample  (Moulis-en-Médoc)

description: 88-90 Barrel sample. Very apply wine, with light botrytis character, some spice and very fresh fruit. It is bright and almost crisp.
title: Château Bastor-Lamontagne 2009 Barrel sample  (Sauternes)

description: Soft, warm and smoky wine, this has a finely layered wood flavor to go with the black currant and red berry fruits. It's dry, yet warm and ripe, finishing with freshness.
title: Château Moulin de Clotte 2009  Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux

description: This medium-weight selection offers concentrated aromas and flavors of black currant and blackberry laced with accents of pepper and earthy spice. There's a bit of bite to the moderate tannins, while hints of tobacco leaf and cocoa nib spice up the finish.
title: Mony 2009 Reserve Dry Red Cabernet Sauvignon (Samson)

description: Pure fruit flavors of cranberry and raspberry, tart and forward, introduce this light and approachable Pinot. Elegant and stylish, without much staying power, it's a good choice for drinking now while your heartier Pinots rest in the cellar.
title: Coelho 2009 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Gigante offers a not-so-giant expression of Chardonnay with nicely measured aromas of stone or passion fruit, pineapple and white almond. The wine is aged in both wood and stainless steel to achieve more length and density on the finish.
title: Gigante 2009 Chardonnay (Colli Orientali del Friuli)

description: In homage to the 150th anniversary of Italian unification, this elegantly designed bottle sports a bright Italian flag on the label. That celebratory spirit continues thanks to the wine's aromas of sweet cherry, cola and raspberry. Its light consistency means it could be consumed slightly chilled with cod or fish soup.
title: Il Vino dei Padri 2009 150 Anniversario Ruché (Monferrato)

description: A little off in texture, with a grainy mouthfeel that's part tart acids and part edgy tannins. However, it's lusty in forward cherry and raspberry fruit flavors, and will surely satisfy many Pinotphiles.
title: Morgan 2009 Twelve Clones Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: A big Pinot Noir, extracted and thick in cherry and blueberry pie filling, red currant, sandalwood and dusty spice flavors. Delicious, but soft and atypical for a Pinot, lacking some life and charm. Drink now.
title: Andretti 2009 Pinot Noir (Carneros)

description: From the Stonetree Vineyard, this supersaturated wine is so black that it looks impenetrable. The dark cassis fruit is drenched in astringent, tongue-drying flavors of smoke, asphalt and tar. Though true to its variety, it's a tough, chewy, somewhat chalky mouthful.
title: Covington 2009 Petite Sirah (Wahluke Slope)

description: This Viognier is a combination of peach and honeyed spice on the nose and palate. Refreshing but with a round, fragrant curve, the wine has a velvety texture and finishes with a spin of peach and tropical fruit.
title: Skouras 2009 Cuvée Larsinos Viognier (Peloponnese)

description: This Pinot Grigio is crisply acidic and dry, with exotic flavors of yellow apricots, tangerines and spicy golden mangoes. It shows toasty, caramelized notes. Refreshing now as a cocktail sipper.
title: Laird 2009 Cold Creek Pinot Grigio (Los Carneros)

description: This wine varies in quality and perception based on when in its lifespan you taste it. Now, toward the end of the line, it's tropical and hanging in there, with green apple, citrus and other typical SB aromas and flavors. Even with 15% Carmenère in the blend for kicks, it's standard and workmanlike.
title: oops 2009 Sauvignon Blanc (Central Valley)

description: Quiet on the bouquet except for a dusting of oak and banana. The palate is heavy and wide, if a bit flabby, and the flavors of melon and mango run sweet, much like fruit punch. Soft and sweet on the finish as well, and overall it's good but generic. Not many Chards in the price range will fare better.
title: Concha y Toro 2009 Xplorador Chardonnay (Central Valley)

description: Bright garnet in color, this delicate wine has aromas of cassis, sour cherry and violet, plus a drying touch of mineral. The mouthfeel is lean, fresh and sharp.
title: Duca di Salaparuta 2009 Vajasindi Nawàri Pinot Nero (Sicilia)

description: In this one-dimensional wine, short-lived sensations of stewed black fruit quickly give way to acrid notes of unsweetened espresso, farmyard, sweat and green coffee beans alongside rough, grainy tannins. A bitterly tannic close leaves the tongue feeling parched.
title: Bonacchi 2009 Molino della Suga  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: A blend of typical Gascon grapes gives a lemon and citrus wine, very fresh, tangy. There is plenty of acidity and an attractive lightness. Screwcap.
title: Lionel Osmin & Cie 2009 Villa Beaurivage White (Vin de France)

description: Purple in color, with grapey aromas that lack complexity. The palate is deep and dense but edgy, with sugary flavors of candied black plum and black cherry. Finishes sweet like burnt brown sugar, with some coffee and anisette. Syrupy but generous.
title: Chilensis 2009 Reserva Malbec (Maule Valley)

description: Rustic and full bodied, this Oregon Primitivo is plenty ripe, yet it retains nuances of olive and herb, along with thick red fruit. A whisper of smoke weaves through the finish.
title: Pheasant Valley Winery 2009 Primitivo (Columbia Valley (OR))

description: With generic berry, cherry and chocolate flavors that are rich and ripe, this soft, simple red blend is O.K. for everyday drinking.
title: Frenchie Winery 2009 Napoleon Red (Sonoma County-Napa County)

description: A difficult wine to assess. It's so ripe and forward in sweet cherry jam and smoky, caramelized oak that it seems like a mere fruit bomb at first sip. But something about the structure goes well beyond simplicity. Could develop bottle complexity over the years, but it's unpredictable.
title: Kokomo 2009 Gopher Hill Block Peters Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Tasting kind of sweet for a Syrah, this has flavors of blackberry jam, coffee and spice.
title: Adelaida 2009 Anna's Vineyard Syrah (Paso Robles)

description: This has bright aromas of maraschino, tobacco, bitter chocolate, prune and dusty mineral. There's a tightness to the mouthfeel that is reinforced by acidity and long-lasting berry flavors.
title: Terre del Principe 2009 Ambruco Pallagrello (Terre del Volturno)

description: A structured wine, with some tight black cherry flavors as well as firm tannins. The wine has a mineral edge, the acidity giving a juicy character to the fruit, while the texture is dry, tannic. Age for a year.
title: Gaël Martin 2009 Les Fouillouses  (Juliénas)

description: Soft and slightly buttery, this smooth-drinking Pinot Gris deftly mixes spiced pear fruit with a pat of butter and a whiff of resin. It's at a perfect point of drinkability right now.
title: Firesteed 2009 Pinot Gris (Oregon)

description: From this rich vintage comes this delicious, open wine. Flavors of tropical fruit and peach create an opulent and generous feel, with notes from having aged in wood offering complexity. It's concentrated and ageworthy, worth keeping for 4–5 years and more.
title: La Chablisienne 2009 Les Preuses Grand Cru  (Chablis)

description: Coming from a single block of the winery's estate vineyard, it has notes of cranberry, black pepper, herbs and moist earth speckled with oak spices (30% new French). The palate has soft, sleek cherry flavors buffeted by firm tannins.
title: Abacela 2009 Reserve South Face Block Syrah (Umpqua Valley)

description: Hints of wet granite and tobacco lend a savory note to tart cranberry and cherry flavors in this earthy offering. Full bodied in style with layers of soft, fine-grained tannins, it has a bold earthy and leathery finish.
title: Palmer 2009 Proprietor's Reserve Cabernet Franc (North Fork of Long Island)

description: A soft, gluey Pinot Noir, with almost no structure and bizarre cherry candy and medicinal flavors.
title: Pietra Santa 2009 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Cienega Valley)

description: An impressive Cabernet, rich and dense in fruit and oak, with tremendous tannins that are at once hard and soft. It's already throwing some sediment, and the cherry and raspberry fruit comes through in a pure, sweetly feminine manner. Should continue to evolve in the bottle for at least 10 years.
title: Cobblestone 2009 Cobblestone Vineyard Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Atlas Peak)

description: Chocolate milk, with a squeeze of cassis liqueur and the sweet vanillins of toasted oak, are the flavors. With the high alcohol, it's almost a dessert wine. Give it credit for a heady opulence.
title: Cerro Prieto 2009 La Teresa Bloc Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: Dark and concentrated but simultaneously fresh and juicy, this blend of 65% Syrah and 35% Grenache offers heady notes of black cherry, plum and raspberry accented by hints of game and tree bark. The meaty, medium-weight palate offers a firm structure and a solid finish.
region_1: Coteaux du Languedoc Pic Saint Loup
title: Château de Lancyre 2009 Vieilles Vignes Red (Coteaux du Languedoc Pic Saint Loup)

description: Dark chocolate and rich plum sauce add complexity to this black fruit-driven Merlot. Blackberry preserves and soft plum guts fill the mouth, with a touch of green pepper appearing on the finish. Medium weight with a creamy texture and a medium-length finish. Drink now.
title: Domaines Astruc 2009 Reserve Merlot (Vin de Pays d'Oc)

description: Tastes a little simple and candied, but it's so delicious, you can hardly stop sipping. Rich and ripe in cherry and raspberry Lifesaver fruit and pie filling, with plenty of sweet vanilla and spice. Drink now.
title: Presqu'ile 2009 Rim Rock Vineyard Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)

description: This blend of Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon, which is only sold in Bermuda, shows the tang of anise alongside cranberry, dried oregano and pencil lead on the nose. The palate's boysenberry fruit is leveled with black pepper, roasting herbs and a long finish that's quite intriguing.
title: Wild Hogge 2009 Tree Frog Cabernet Sauvignon-Syrah (Paso Robles)

description: A simple, fruity wine that features ample pink grapefruit and gooseberry aromas and flavors, this is a bit of a one-trick pony, but it delivers those bold fruit flavors with authority. Drink up.
title: Sileni 2009 Cellar Selection Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: This Zinfandel takes time to unlayer, but once it does, the combination of leather and blackberry is tasty, and the finish is pleasantly spicy.
title: Zinfandelic 2009 Old Vine Zinfandel (Amador County)

description: A simple, slightly sweet wine, with candied berry, sugar and spice flavors. Drink it at picnics, parties, the beach.
designation: R Collection Lot No 7 Field Blend
title: Raymond 2009 R Collection Lot No 7 Field Blend Red (California)

description: Elegant, crisp wine from a single vineyard, edged with minerality as well as ripe pear and pineapple flavors. There is a spicy character with touches of almonds and tense acidity. Age for several years.
title: Stadlmann 2009 Mandel-Höh Zierfandler (Thermenregion)

description: An impressive, ageworthy Riesling. It shows great depth of flavor, its intense, biting acidity shooting through the green apple and plum fruits. Very nervy, minerally and taut. Designed for longterm aging.
title: Forstreiter 2009 Schiefer Reserve Riesling (Kremstal)

description: A rosé of Sangiovese from southern Tuscany (Montalcino), Lo Sbrancato features ripe aromas of wild berry, cranberry and white almond. It's a simple, fresh wine that would pair well with mussels steamed in cream sauce.
title: Il Poggione 2009 Lo Sbrancato Rosé (Toscana)

description: Mostly Merlot, with some Cabernet Sauvignon, this blend starts out a bit angular, so give it some air before drinking. Although it's not an ager, it shows pleasantly ripe blackberry, cherry, mocha, herb and spice flavors.
title: J Ludlow 2009 Jane Red (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Pinot Noir specialist Rick Longoria continues to tackle this difficult variety, which may be the hardest to master in California. His results are inconsistent. The '09 is a very good wine, bone dry and dusty, with tobacco and cherry flavors.
title: Longoria 2009 Clover Creek Vineyard Tempranillo (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Aromas of underbrush, blackberry, ground black pepper and a whiff of leather come together in the glass. The warm, forward palate shows dried black cherry, licorice, clove and sage accompanied by dusty tannins that leave a drying finish. Drink sooner rather than later to catch the remaining fruit richness.
title: Montemercurio 2009 Messaggero  (Vino Nobile di Montepulciano)

description: Black cherry fruit is accented with flavors of coffee grounds and dark chocolate. The fruit is clean and brambly, and the wine overdelivers for the price.
title: Hyatt 2009 Cabernet Sauvignon (Rattlesnake Hills)

description: Foxy cherry and cough drop aromas are not convincing. Feels astringent and tastes tart, with sharp but candied cherry, faux oak and milk chocolate flavors. Blends sour notes with sweet oak, all to little avail.
designation: Family Vineyards Organic Legacy Old Vines Single Vineyard
title: Caligiore 2009 Family Vineyards Organic Legacy Old Vines Single Vineyard Malbec (Mendoza)

description: Plush, full and warm, this is an immensely attractive wine now, though it should cellar well through 2021. Dark, intense notes of berry preserves, menthol, curing spice and vanilla bean all mingle harmoniously on the nose and palate. Lush and mouthfilling, it is carried by a full structure and a crushed-velvet texture. Notes of muddled black berries and sweet spice linger long on the finish.
title: Kleinood 2009 Tamboerskloof Syrah (Stellenbosch)

description: Bold, thick and chewy, this masculine blend of Montepulciano and Cabernet Franc delivers lasting aromas of pipe tobacco, cinnamon, jammy blackberry and dried herb. The finish is driven by a soft, chocolate-fudge-like aftertaste.
title: Tenuta Vitalonga 2009 Phi Natura Armonia Red (Umbria)

description: A lovely Merlot, this is pure, vibrant and elegant. It has classic cherry, red-currant and cedar flavors, and while there's a sweetness at the core, the finish is thoroughly dry. Wonderful to drink now with steak or lamb.
title: Duckhorn 2009 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: Chardonnay in name only. It's a simple, watery wine, with sweet apricot, tangerine and faux oak flavors.
title: Esser 2009 Chardonnay (Monterey)

description: Slender and simple, this has a green asparagus flavor that thins the citrus and pear notes.
title: Farrier 2009 Andiron Semillon-Sauvignon Blanc (Alexander Valley)

description: Marred by a vegetal taste, this has hot, thin cherry and oak flavors. The blend is Mourvèdre, Zinfandel and Grenache.
title: Minassian-Young 2009 Junipero Red (Paso Robles)

description: There's a slight coppery tinge to this wine's hue, which can't help but influence perceptions of aroma and flavor. With 7 g/l of residual sugar, this is off-dry in style, with strawberry, apple and melon flavors. Like an alcohol-infused fruit cocktail with a sense of balance and proportion.
title: Awatere River 2009 Pinot Gris (Marlborough)

description: This is a candied, slightly sweet Pinot Noir, pretty rustic all around. Its acidity and sugary finish give it a raspberry and cherry, sweet and sour taste.
title: Francis Coppola 2009 Director's Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Lush and luscious, this is a standout among the 2009 Erath Pinots. The richness of the fruit gives this zaftig wine immense appeal, with deep cherry flavors streaked with gun metal, anise, mocha, and coffee. A lick of dark chocolate ensures a glorious finish.
title: Erath 2009 Prince Hill Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: Striking acidity adds freshness to more rustic characteristics of cooked red fruit, caramelized sugar and hay on this medium-bodied Pinot. Brambly on the palate with hints of mushroom and foliage, it finishes clean with just a trace of tannins.
title: Inspire Moore 2009 LUST Pinot Noir (Finger Lakes)

description: Delicate sour cherry and plum flavors are intensified by notes of black coffee, fresh green herb and aromatic cedar. This medium-bodied Pinot Noir is earthy and savory, with refreshingly high acidity.
title: Lakewood 2009 Pinot Noir (Finger Lakes)

description: This is a richly ripe 2009 Pouilly Fumé. It steers right to the tropical fruit end of the spectrum while not losing sight of a more textured mineral character. It is rich and could age for 1–2 years and more.
title: Château de Tracy 2009  Pouilly-Fumé

description: A bold and modern expression of Nebbiolo, this shows bright, pulpy fruit and pretty aromas of blackberry, crushed clove and earthy, rusty tones. There's a touch of bitter almond on the finish and loads of elegant fruit.
title: Ca'Romè 2009 Sorì Rio Sordo  (Barbaresco)

description: This is too ripe, with a disagreeable pruny taste. The wine is dry and full bodied, but is almost like a minor Port. The official alcohol reading is 14.8% by volume, but it tastes higher and hotter than that.
title: Heart O' The Mountain 2009 Estate Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)

description: This is an herbal Merlot that tastes of toasty oak and finishes slightly sweet.
title: Cable Car 2009 Merlot (Lodi)

description: Delicate aromas of hawthorn, white spring flower and a whiff of orchard fruit aromas lead the nose on this refined sparkler. The vibrant palate doles yellow apple, citrus zest and toasted breadcrumb accompanied by an elegant mousse.
title: La Valle 2009 Naturalis Extra Brut Sparkling (Franciacorta)

description: Now fully mature and showing some bricking at the rim, this is a savory, earthy example of Marlborough Pinot Noir. Roasted cauliflower and caramelized root veggies sound strange but are actually quite pleasant, while hints of dried fruit and molasses add the suggestion of fruit sweetness. Drink now.
title: Foxes Island 2009 Belsham Awatere Estate Pinot Noir (Marlborough)

description: Aromas of French oak, pressed rose, wild berry, mint, new leather and a whiff of truffle slowly unfold in the glass. The lean, evolved palate offers dried black cherry, espresso and toasted oak alongside drying, fleeting tannins that leave an astringent finish. Drink sooner rather than later to catch the remaining fruit.
title: Tenuta Rocca 2009 Riserva San Pietro  (Barolo)

description: This toasty expression of Amarone delivers espresso, subtle oak, plum cake and smoky aromas. On the palate, notes of tobacco leaf and grilled herbs accent the primary raisin and prune flavors alongside tightly packed tannins that need a few more years to unwind. Drink after 2017.
title: Masi 2009 Costasera Riserva  (Amarone della Valpolicella Classico)

description: Smooth and rich, with ripe, spiced peach as well as a mineral edge. The acidity tightens the generous texture of this powerful wine, leaving the concentration deliciously fresh.
title: Markus Huber 2009 Alte Setzen Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Traisental)

description: A delicious Zinfandel, one of Fritz's best in years. It shows Zin's robust, briary character, with a peppery, brambly mouthfeel. Yet the long, cool vintage has resulted in sweetly ripe flavors of wild berries.
title: Fritz 2009 Estate Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)

description: For maximum enjoyment, drink this almost all-Merlot blend now. It shows ripe, lush black-cherry, red currant, plum and bacon flavors that finish spicy and sweet tasting. The tannins are thick but smooth.
title: Terlato 2009 Angels' Peak Merlot-Cabernet Franc (Napa Valley)

description: Rich, full bodied and smooth, this wine is driven by its black fruits and dark tannins. Acidity lines the fruitiness, which gives a juicy edge to the solid texture. It shows potential, so keep it for at least four years.
title: Campolargo 2009 Valdazar Red (Bairrada)

description: The name refers to the blend of 62% Barbera and 38% Nebbiolo sourced from a pair of Horse Heaven Hills vineyards. A little high-toned on the nose, the wine eventually smoothes out in the glass, and it offers pretty flavors of raspberry and Bing cherry. Succulent and nicely balanced, this is one of the better Washington examples of these two grapes.
title: Tre Nova 2009 Nebbaro Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This blend is comprised of seven varieties that were sourced from nine vineyards. Despite a hint of green in the tannins and a bit of stem in the fruits, this shows good complexity. Streaks of earth, mineral and licorice mingle around notes of dried cherry and fig.
title: Walla Walla Vintners 2009 Washington State Cuvée Red (Washington)

description: A big but not-too-tannic Petit Verdot, this is juicy and structured with red fruit and a light layer of spice. Tending toward black licorice and dark chocolate, it could use a splash of something to brighten it up. But it's otherwise a bottle worth trying if you're curious to try a varietal Petit Verdot.
title: Concannon 2009 Reserve Petit Verdot (Livermore Valley)

description: Delicious flavors of lemons, limes, tangerines and honey in this wine, with notes of flowers and steel. It's also very acidic and off dry. A simple Riesling, but a clean and attractive one.
title: Santa Barbara Winery 2009 Lafond Vineyard Riesling (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Rich and round but not overdone, with a nice brightness to the apple, clementine and white peach aromas and flavors. Balanced and well-integrated, with all of the components working together in harmony. Closes on a brisk citrus-rind finish. Drink now–2014.
title: Hartenberg 2009 Chardonnay (Stellenbosch)

description: An awkward wine, whose blackberry flavors are marred by vegetal notes.
title: Croad 2009 Estate Bottled Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)

description: Rounded and sweet, this is a rich Gewurztraminer that's full of spice and textured with coconut. It is full in the mouth—a wine for rich sauces and pâtés.
title: Domaines Schlumberger 2009 Les Princes Abbés Gewurztraminer (Alsace)

description: Young and strong now, this has lots of primary berry and chocolate flavors. The oak hasn't been integrated into the wine, making it feel rustic, with an emphasis on the tannins and acids. Give it two or three years before opening, but not much more than that.
title: Newton 2009 Unfiltered Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Tight and scattered right out of the bottle, marked by acidity, fresh fruit, firm tannins and sweet oak. The balance seems to be there for this wine to develop some admirable qualities about 5–6 years down the road.
title: Lafond 2009 Arita Hills Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: A fine blend, showing bright acidity and classic citrus and tropical fruit flavors, with a firm minerality. Oak brings a sweet edge of buttered toast and vanilla. Drink now.
title: Bjornstad 2009 Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

description: This rich, full-bodied wine is becoming smooth and ripe. The tannins are enveloped by the generous black fruits and drier raisins. A core of dryness show the wine is still developing. Drink from 2015.
title: Château Laborde 2009 Cuvée 1628  (Lalande de Pomerol)

description: This brings light flavors of leaf and hard cherry candy, with a hint of cola.After its quick start, the wine tails off gently.
title: Maryhill 2009 Proprietor's Reserve Grenache (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: For whatever reason, this wine consistently fails to impress. Once again, it's vegetal and pickled, with forced flavors of candy and too much residual sugar. Forget it if you only drink dry rosé.
title: Miguel Torres 2009 Santa Digna Reserve Rosé Cabernet Sauvignon (Central Valley)

description: 97-99 Barrel sample. A wonderfully structured wine,it combines impressive purity of fruit with delicious, mouth-watering acidity, The dry structure holds this elegant structure perfectly in place. A great success.
title: Château Mouton Rothschild 2009 Barrel sample  (Pauillac)

description: Fragrant of spiced apples and yellow florals, this dry, medium- bodied Gewürztraminer is sunny and bright on the palate with high-toned apple and honey flavors.
title: Knapp 2009 Sunrise Hill Vineyard Dry Gewürztraminer (Cayuga Lake)

description: Savory and meaty on first take, with ample berry on the nose as warmth and leather seep through. The palate is round and healthy, with a mix of succulent berry and savory spice and leather pumping up the flavor profile. Spicy and chocolaty on the finish.
title: Notro 2009 Malbec (Mendoza)

description: Aromatically restrained, with hints of green herbs, underripe apple and pineapple on the nose. It's semisweet in style, but brisk and approachable with pretty apple blossom and tart, green apple-skin flavors on the palate.
title: Treleaven 2009 Semi-Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: A blend of Cataratto and Grillo, Cariddi is a fresh and easy white wine with mild tones of citrus, stone fruit and pear. Pair it with raw fish or smoked salmon.
title: Colosi 2009 Cariddi White (Sicilia)

description: Much higher production than Noceto's Riserva and sourced more broadly, this Sangiovese is more rustic and earthy, its cranberry flavors bursting from the seams, and would be yummy with thin-crust pizza.
title: Noceto 2009 Sangiovese (Amador County)

description: Tastes like a sweet and sour sauce mixed into white wine, with pineapple, sugar and lime flavors and an alcoholic buzz.
title: One Hope 2009 Sauvignon Blanc (California)

description: This simple, easy-going Nero d'Avola opens with a slightly burnt or charred characteristic that recalls asphalt and rubber tire. Wait a few minutes for the red fruit and almond to come through.
title: Feudo Maccari 2009 Nero d'Avola (Sicilia)

description: A bit on the sugary sweet side, but otherwise this is a nice cocktail sipper. Shows good acidity coupled with pineapple, Meyer lemon, lime, green apple and spice flavors. Would score much higher if it were drier.
title: Field Stone 2009 Redwood Ranch Sauvignon Blanc (Alexander Valley)

description: A dry wine, companion to the winery's off-dry estate bottling. This does not give up its fruit as some dry Rieslings do; there is a pleasing mix of lemon, grapefruit, pineapple and apricot, full in the mid-palate, then turning more astringent and cheerily tart in the finish. This is very young, compact, and still tight as a drum; it could be cellared for up to a decade.
title: Trisaetum 2009 Josahn Riesling (Willamette Valley)

description: This Syrah-Grenache blend is enormously rich, ripe and heady. It wears its 16% abv well, without ever feeling hot or awkward. Instead, fine acidity and firm tannins give the dark chocolate and blackberry jam flavors the balanced lusciousness of Port, without the sweetness.
title: Clos Solène 2009 Hommage à nos Pairs Reserve Syrah-Grenache (Paso Robles)

description: This round and fruity blend mixes fruit from three vineyards: Lange Estate, Freedom Hill and Yamhill Vineyard. Ripe black fruits matched to firm, dusty tannins are highlighted with streaks of sassafras and underscored with vivid acidity.
title: Lange 2009 Three Hills Cuvée Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Whiffs of juicy melon and yellow peach brighten darker notes of potpourri and aged honey on this very pleasant late-harvest Riesling. Quite hefty on the palate with hints of dusty minerals and a sharp, clean acidity.
title: White Springs 2009 Late Harvest Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Grown primarily in Chile these days, Carmenère can be akin to Merlot, though this version has the herbal qualities of a Cab Franc and is otherwise quite oaky.
title: Yorkville Cellars 2009 Rennie Vineyard Carmenère (Yorkville Highlands)

description: A pretty mineral note appears at the very beginning of this Sangiovese-Merlot-Cabernet Sauvignon blend and is followed by ripe fruit flavors and a touch of caper or green olive. The wine is rich and plush, with a long-lasting finish.
title: Le Grascete 2009 Grale  (Bolgheri)

description: Winemaker Laurent Montalieu has outdone himself with the fruit from this heritage vineyard. Silky and deep, with a luscious mix of berries, it's a supple, seductive Pinot, wrapped in sleek layers of spicy, toasty oak.
title: Hyland 2009 Pinot Noir (McMinnville)

description: The 2009 vintage was successful for Sausal's three new Zinfandels, and this intense, concentrated bottling shows how the cool weather managed to ripen the grapes, yet keep alcohol levels moderate. It's a big, powerful wine, packed with wild berry, cola, licorice, spice and sandalwood flavors that have a tang of the earth.
title: Sausal 2009 Century Vines Zinfandel (Alexander Valley)

description: A good followup to last year's bottling, although the wine isn't quite as ripe and lush. The cool vintage has brought out a tannic earthiness, as well as a blast of black pepper that makes the blackberry and blueberry fruit tingly on the tongue. It's a complicated, individualistic wine, and you might want to cellar it for up to six years and see what happens.
title: Aril 2009 Syrah (Atlas Peak)

description: A simple Zinfandel, this is lacking in structure and balance, with overripe and underripe flavors. The basic taste is of spicy, oaky blackberries, with a Porty heat from the high alcohol.
title: Trentadue 2009 La Storia Zinfandel (Alexander Valley)

description: Aromas of cherry and white chocolate with a touch of shale open to flavors of strawberry, pomegranate and mocha with a splash of ouzo. A web of soft tannins and symmetry between fruit and sophisticated herbal notes lead to a pleasantly bright finish.
title: Golan Heights Winery 2009 Yarden Pinot Noir (Galilee)

description: This vino naturale (the wine is not filtered and has no added sulfites) opens with intense aromas of butterscotch, candied fruit, apricot and honey. The blend is Chardonnay, Sauvignon and Malvasia, and it tastes slightly oxidized overall.
title: Paraschos 2009 Ponka White (Venezia Giulia)

description: Shy aromas of cherries, peppery herbs, char and spiced mocha lead to a light- to medium-bodied palate that feels silky and slick. Bright acidity gives the finish some lift, and it ends on an accent of cherry lozenge.
title: Fox Meadow 2009 Reserve Cabernet Franc (Virginia)

description: Green, mildly vegetal and lightly tropical, with a tangy, acid-driven palate and citrusy, sharp flavors of grapefruit, tangerine and orange. Quick on the finish but sizzling, with an abundance of mouth-washing acidity. Very food friendly even toward the end of its prescribed lifespan.
title: Caliterra 2009 Tributo Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

description: Flashy and flamboyant, but smells and tastes overoaked, with buttered toast, caramel and vanilla meringue notes swamping the underlying tangerine, lemon and mango fruit. Drink now.
title: Dutton Estate 2009 Dutton Palms Vineyard Dutton Ranch Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: With mineral and apple on the nose, this qualifies as a pure and pleasing SB for almost any occasion. The palate is elegant and lithe, while the flavors of green apple, gooseberry and grapefruit are what they should be. Pure, sound and solid wine to drink right now.
title: Casa Silva 2009 Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Colchagua Valley)

description: A bit resiny and hard at first, with apple and a lot of oak on a compact, somewhat neutral nose. It's a heavy wine in the mouth, with oak on top of lemon, apple and green banana flavors. Finishes resiny, with odd spice notes. Viura, Malvasia and Garnacha Blanca is the mix.
title: Sierra Cantabria 2009 Organza White (Rioja)

description: Dark, alluring aromas of licorice, blackberry and toast share space with earthy, mossy notes on the nose of this full-bodied winner that's showing wall-to-wall weight and tannins. Black plum, cassis and tobacco flavors are a common Rioja trifecta, as are dry, peppery, figgy flavors on the finish. Drink from 2018–2028.
title: Bodegas Landaluce 2009 Capricho  (Rioja)

description: Sweet and extracted in pineapple, tangerine, lime and vanilla flavors, this Chardonnay also is generously oaked, which gives it even richer vanilla and buttered toast notes. It's flashy, yet would benefit from greater dryness and restraint. Size isn't everything.
title: Anaba 2009 Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: This is proof that vintage variation is alive and well in Argentina. Reflecting a hot year, this is stewy and heavy. It smells of licorice, mint and black fruit, while the palate is syrupy. Prune, raisin and stewed blackberry flavors are front and center.
title: Benegas 2009 Libertad Vineyards Malbec (Mendoza)

description: A big, rich, softly jammy Petite Sirah that's packed with flavors of blackberry, blueberry, dark chocolate, anise and pepper. It's tannic, so cellaring this puppy for a good six years is recommended—although, it will always be fairly rustic.
title: Frank Family 2009 SJ Vineyard Petite Sirah (Napa Valley)

description: There's a lot of fruit to this New World Syrah. Cherry, cassis and berry aromas are fresh and forward, while in the mouth, this is full but not overly demanding or exact. Flavors of cherry and plum come with creamy oak notes of vanilla, while the finish is crisp in feel but generic in flavor.
title: Santa Rita 2009 Reserva Syrah (Maipo Valley)

description: This Syrah is somewhat tough and gritty in tannins, with a bite of acidity. It's very dry, with flavors of tobacco, herbs, blackberries and cola. A good, sound full-bodied red for drinking now.
title: Cline 2009 Syrah (Sonoma County)

description: The Crossings continues to be a reliable source of Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc at a reasonable price. The 2009 seems almost sweet in its intensity of ripe nectarine fruit, but there's enough grapefruit for balance and just a trace of herb on the finish.
title: The Crossings 2009 Sauvignon Blanc (Awatere Valley)

description: The aromas of black fruit, black olive and herb are solid. Racy and tangy due to pushy acidity, this has berry, herb and vanilla flavors. Fresh in feel but basic in flavor.
title: Botalcura 2009 La Porfia Gran Reserva Carmenère (Maule Valley)

description: This has slender raspberry and cherry fruit. As a result, the acidity dominates, giving the wine a sour candy tartness. In its favor, it's nicely dry, and it feels silky.
title: Casa de la Cruz 2009 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Soft and sweet, a simple country-style wine with cherry jam flavors as well as a streak of wintergreen mint.
title: Curtis 2009 Mourvèdre (Santa Barbara County)

description: With a beautiful purple color, this wine quickly exhibits classic Cabernet aromas of black currants and cedar. In the mouth, it's full bodied and concentrated, showing powerful, sweet blackberry fruit. It's incredibly refined, impressively structured, massive, impeccable and frankly delicious. This should develop over the next 20 years, at least.
title: Ovid 2009 Red (Napa Valley)

description: An awesome wine—this is so massively intense in blackberry, blueberry and cassis flavors, it's hard to believe. From the first sip though the middle palate, it's seemingly sweet with a fruity essence, but the finish is dry and firm. Drink now and for 8–10 years, at the very least.
title: Von Strasser 2009 Agira Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Diamond Mountain District)

description: Made from Sierra Foothills Zinfandel, this smells of grapes dried in the sun. This is delicately balanced, with just the right level of thickness and sweetness. Enjoy with plummy desserts or dark chocolate.
title: Easton 2009 Late-Harvest Obscura Zinfandel (Fiddletown)
